reviews.review_title: Made me scream more than once!
reviews.review_description: I was impressed by this app.  Even though the graphics aren’t 4K, it doesn’t matter because it will make you jump more than once.  Also it wasn’t one of those 3 minute runs.  It actually lasted over 8 minutes or so.  Give it a try you won’t be disappointed!
I absolutely enjoyed this, a fair few scares along the way, I would have happily paid for it, but it was FREE, thank you. Well worth the download :-)
Not be on it yet but the reviews look good most of the reviews yes it was free i pay for it so touch wood it's going to be good
Although still good how bout improveing the graphics and details some more?
Fun. Not scary but still fun.
Now you made me jump more than once! Loved it!
Terror Cave stopped unexpectedly all the time it won't go past the home screen
Bet my neighbours where wondering what I was screaming about!! Loved it

It was slow and not too scary. Very reminiscent of a Boardwalk haunted house ride. You go in hoping to get scared to death and walk out with just a few decent startles under your belt.

I think this is good if you're looking for "kid level" scary versus actual horror. Nothing nightmare inducing, but still mildly entertaining.

As an adult I think I'll pass on a round two, but I'd still check it out if the developers come up with anything else.

I played the free version and then decided to upgrade. There are several points where I jumped out of my seat. VR makes the experiences much more scary

reviews.review_title: Not bad, especially for horror newbies
reviews.review_description:
One day while just randomly browsing through the vast Go library, I just so happened to stumble upon the realization that Polyraw was the developer responsible for what looked like three pretty cool experiences: Terror Cave, Relax River, and Dream Journey. Considering that all three of these titles were reasonably priced, I just went ahead and purchased them all sight unseen. Almost like a trilogy, if you will. As such, this review will act as one part of a three-part review.

Terror Cave looked like, at first glance, kind of lame. I was worried it was just going to be an underwhelming kiddie-carnival-type ride where the only thing scary is the time slowly ticking off your watch until it’s over. I was pleasantly surprised to see that it actually had some real merit to it. For one thing, the graphics are a bit better than what they may initially seem. Nothing groundbreaking, but good enough to instill some sense of mood and atmosphere. As for the scares, it won’t give you nightmares or sleepless nights. It will, however, at times startle the dickens out of you and leave you at least a tiny bit frazzled. One particular moment, that I will not spoil for you, got me so good that I actually clapped and said “Bravo.” There are two modes to this experience: a version with all of the scares, and one without. The latter is more of just the spooky atmosphere that younger kids can get slightly creeped out by and enjoy. In short, this is a slightly above-average horror experience for those whose tolerance for scares falls on the lower side. Horror enthusiasts (including me) need to remind ourselves that there’s nothing wrong with that, and that not every horror title needs to be all-out disturbing. Keeping that in mind, Terror Cave comes recommended at this price point. For horror newbies and lightweights, this is your title.

reviews.review_title: Terror cave HD and terror cave lit
reviews.review_description: Have had these two apps for 7 months and recently they stopped playing don't know why any advice

This is pretty good but a few things I would like to see fixed. 1 get rid of the satanic pentagrams. 2 make the real dark scenes brighter cause Oculus Go doesn't handle darkness very well. 3 improve the graphics and resolution.

For 79p you can’t go wrong... it had a few jumps in it, some family members tried it... some screamed some didn’t! It was pretty good and I will definitely use it to show people the VR - graphics aren’t great but hey, it’s 79p. I did up the game and buy Affected - the manor which is a lot more intense if you want even more of a scare. Overall this was still a good purchase!
Fantastic game.  It made me jump a few times.
